text.tspan: Solve PYQs and topic-wise questions, save your progress,
track daily goals, and know exactly what to revise next.
Built for aspirants who want clarity, not clutter.

g.text: Practice volume that feels real from day one.
Users understand what to do the moment they land.
Small sessions that fit real prep routines.
4 more questions to hit today’s target.
Which Article deals with the Right to Education?

text.tspan: The homepage should sell the loop,
Aspirants need to feel three things fast: this is serious, this is simple,
and this will help me study consistently.
The hero is about practice, progress,
Exam chips, one main CTA, and visible
daily-goal value reduce hesitation in the

svg.text: PrepBros should feel like a focused command center for daily prep.

text.tspan: Not a marketplace, not a coaching portal, and not a feature museum.

svg.text: A warmer, more premium study product look

g.text: Clear numbers, one message, no clutter.
Show real product behavior on the homepage.
Daily goal and weak-topic visuals should feel native.
Serious but modern. Avoid generic SaaS white cards.

text.tspan: You do not need to show every feature.
You need to make the core loop feel

svg.text: Simplified V1 navigation shown inside the concept

text.tspan: Keep trust links here, not in the hero.

svg.text: Homepage concept for a sharper PrepBros V1

text.tspan: This direction keeps the brand serious, premium, and exam-focused while making the core loop obvious:
start practicing, save progress, review weak areas, come back daily.
